title: rapistrum hypogaeum
intro: Rapistrum hypogaeum is a flowering plant species in the Brassicaceae family, also known as the rape or wild turnip. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which are attributed to its volatile oils and glycosides. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues, as well as being used as an antiseptic and diuretic. Herbal preparations of Rapistrum hypogaeum, such as infusions, decoctions, and poultices, are typically used in folk medicine to treat various ailments.
